Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Dmitry, the "pure" cross-compile stuff of your patch looks ok to me, even if I did not have time to heavily test it. But what I don't understand is the presence of changes in lmv_obd.c and cl_lock.c ?? If it is for an other bug, they should be pushed as a separate patch.

Patch Set 4:

I'm not sure why CROSS_COMPILE is needed in lustre makefiles, --host should be setting CC/.. to appropriate compiler ?

Patch Set 4:

Quote from Kernel Makefile:
# CROSS_COMPILE specify the prefix used for all executables used
# during compilation. Only gcc and related bin-utils executables
# are prefixed with $(CROSS_COMPILE).
# CROSS_COMPILE can be set on the command line
# make CROSS_COMPILE=ia64-linux-
# Alternatively CROSS_COMPILE can be set in the environment.
# Default value for CROSS_COMPILE is not to prefix executables

# Make variables (CC, etc...)

AS              = $(CROSS_COMPILE)as
LD              = $(CROSS_COMPILE)ld
CC              = $(CROSS_COMPILE)gcc
CPP             = $(CC) -E
AR              = $(CROSS_COMPILE)ar
NM              = $(CROSS_COMPILE)nm
STRIP           = $(CROSS_COMPILE)strip
OBJCOPY         = $(CROSS_COMPILE)objcopy
OBJDUMP         = $(CROSS_COMPILE)objdump

Patch Set 4:

i do understand why you define CROSS_COMPILE itself, but why do you use it in genlib.sh ?  it should be already defined properly by configure means.

Patch Set 4:

We have tools declarations with absolute path in our genlib.sh. I just fix this with common approach by using CROSS_COMPILE prefix. Probably we can just remove those declarations from genlib.sh.

Patch Set 4:

would be nice to try, imo. going to test the patch with os/x as well.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 5:

Remove tools declaration from genlib.sh but was required to pass environment explicitly to script from Makefile because of it's not passed by default.

AR=$(AR) RANLIB=$(RANLIB) sh $(srcdir)/genlib.sh "$(LIBS)" "$(LND_LIBS)" "$(PTHREAD_LIBS)"

i just checked - i do define CROSS_COMPILE and this seem to be enough.. no ARCH or CROSS_ARCH is needed, AFAICS.

Patch Set 7:

The host and target type will be recorded in the following shell variables:

`host'
The canonical configuration name of the host. This will normally be determined by running the `config.guess' shell script, although the user is permitted to override this by using an explicit `--host' option.
`target'
The canonical configuration name of the target.
...
Note that if `host' and `target' are the same string, you can assume a native configuration. If they are different, you can assume a cross configuration.



so, I'd think we should compare host/target and if they do not match, define CROSS_COMPILE=$CC ?

Patch Set 19: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Just for my info, did you get crashes upon use of the PCLMULQDQ instruction ?

Patch Set 19:

The Intel(R) Xeon Phi(TM) is subset of x86_64 arch and don't have PCLMULQDQ instruction. So, I switch off it in early stage to avoid compilation of assembler error.

Patch Set 21: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It would be better to name the conditional variable HAVE_PCLMULQDQ instead of just HAVE_PCLMUL. The extra 3 letters won't kill anyone, and make it more clear what is actually being checked.

To be honest, the commit comment and config/lustre-build.m4 comment are a little over the top. They should be describing the functionality, not be an advertisement.  I can understand listing the architecture once in the commit comment, but four times is a bit excessive with the (R) and (tm) and such.

parents.subject: LU-2442 kernel: SLES11 performance fixes and updates

commit.subject: LU-2752 build: Enhance build for cross compilation for MIC
commit.message:
LU-2752 build: Enhance build for cross compilation for MIC

Enhance lustre build for cross compilation for the Intel(R) Xeon
Phi(TM) card. In addition to standard build the GNU cross
toolchain for the Intel(R) Xeon Phi(TM) can be used to produce
client binaries for the Intel(R) Xeon Phi(TM) card. To enable
this just specify appropriate --host and --build option for
./configure.

For example, to produce Lustre client binaries for Intel(R) Xeon
Phi(TM) card just execute the following commands:

NOTE: You should have "intel-mic-gpl-<version>.x86_64" package
installed and MIC GPL sources unpacked in /opt/intel/mic/src.

export PATH=/usr/linux-k1om-4.7/bin:$PATH

sh ./autogen.sh

./configure --with-linux=/opt/intel/mic/src/card/kernel \
    --disable-server --without-o2ib \
    --host=x86_64-k1om-linux --build=x86_64-pc-linux

make

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I4347c65f67bd836116532989c2132457f5eee934
